docs.title: pykabutan
docs.text: A Python library for scraping Japanese stock data from kabutan.jp.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Ticker data: Company profiles, historical prices, news, financials, shareholders
  • Search: Find stocks by industry or theme
  • yfinance-style API: Familiar interface for Python developers
  • Lazy loading: HTTP requests only when data is accessed
  • Built-in rate limiting: Respectful scraping with configurable delays

docs.title: Quick Example
docs.text:
import pykabutan as pk

# Get stock data
ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")  # Toyota
print(ticker.profile.name)   # トヨタ自動車
print(ticker.profile.market) # 東証Ｐ

# Get historical prices
df = ticker.history(period="30d")
print(df.head())

# Search by industry
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")
print(f"Found {len(results)} stocks")

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install pykabutan

Or with uv:

uv add pykabutan

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+
  • requests
  • beautifulsoup4
  • pandas
  • lxml

docs.title: License
docs.text: MIT

docs.title: Try it Online
docs.text: No installation needed - try pykabutan directly in Google Colab:

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
Install pykabutan using pip:

pip install pykabutan

Or with uv:

uv add pykabutan

docs.title: Import the library
docs.text: import pykabutan as pk

docs.title: Get stock information
docs.text:
Create a Ticker object with a stock code:

ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")  # Toyota Motor Corporation

Access the company profile:

profile = ticker.profile

print(profile.name)      # トヨタ自動車
print(profile.market)    # 東証Ｐ
print(profile.industry)  # 輸送用機器
print(profile.per)       # Price-to-earnings ratio
print(profile.pbr)       # Price-to-book ratio

docs.title: Get historical prices
docs.text:
# Last 30 days
df = ticker.history(period="30d")

# Last 90 days, weekly data
df = ticker.history(period="90d", interval="week")

# Specific number of rows
df = ticker.history(rows=100)

The returned DataFrame contains:

Column Description date Trading date open Opening price high High price low Low price close Closing price volume Trading volume change Price change from previous day

docs.title: Search for stocks
docs.text:
Search by industry:

# Get all electronics companies
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

for t in results[:5]:
    print(f"{t.code}: {t.profile.name}")

Search by theme:

# Search for AI-related stocks (use Japanese terms)
results = pk.search_by_theme("人工知能")

List available industries:

industries = pk.list_industries()
print(industries)

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Configure request behavior:

# Set timeout (seconds)
pk.config.timeout = 60

# Set delay between requests (seconds)
pk.config.request_delay = 1.0

# Reset to defaults
pk.config.reset()

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
from pykabutan import TickerNotFoundError

try:
    ticker = pk.Ticker("9999999")
    profile = ticker.profile
except TickerNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Ticker not found: {e.code}")

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Ticker Guide - Detailed ticker usage
  • Search Guide - Advanced search options
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation

docs.title: Global Config Object
docs.text:
import pykabutan as pk

config = pk.config

The global config object controls HTTP request behavior.

docs.title: timeout
docs.text:
@property
def timeout(self) -> int

@timeout.setter
def timeout(self, value: int | float) -> None

HTTP request timeout in seconds.

Default: 30

Validation: Must be positive (> 0)

Example:

pk.config.timeout = 60
print(pk.config.timeout)  # 60

docs.title: request_delay
docs.text:
@property
def request_delay(self) -> float

@request_delay.setter
def request_delay(self, value: int | float) -> None

Delay between consecutive HTTP requests in seconds.

Default: 0.5

Validation: Must be non-negative (>= 0)

Example:

pk.config.request_delay = 1.0
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 1.0

docs.title: user_agent
docs.text:
@property
def user_agent(self) -> str

@user_agent.setter
def user_agent(self, value: str) -> None

HTTP User-Agent header.

Default: Chrome browser user agent string

Validation: Must be non-empty string

Example:

pk.config.user_agent = "MyApp/1.0"

docs.title: reset()
docs.text:
def reset(self) -> None

Reset all configuration to default values.

Example:

pk.config.timeout = 120
pk.config.request_delay = 5.0
pk.config.reset()

print(pk.config.timeout)        # 30
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 0.5

docs.title: Validation Errors
docs.text:
Invalid values raise ValueError:

# Timeout must be positive
pk.config.timeout = 0     # ValueError
pk.config.timeout = -1    # ValueError

# Request delay must be non-negative
pk.config.request_delay = -1  # ValueError

# User agent must be non-empty
pk.config.user_agent = ""     # ValueError
pk.config.user_agent = None   # ValueError

docs.title: Type Conversion
docs.text:
Values are automatically converted:

Input Type Property Result float timeout Converted to int int request_delay Converted to float 
pk.config.timeout = 30.7
print(pk.config.timeout)  # 30 (int)

pk.config.request_delay = 2
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 2.0 (float)

docs.title: Default Values Summary
docs.text: Property Default Type timeout 30 int request_delay 0.5 float user_agent Chrome UA str

docs.title: Exception Hierarchy
docs.text:
Exception
└── PykabutanError
    ├── TickerNotFoundError
    ├── ScrapingError
    └── ConfigurationError

All pykabutan exceptions inherit from PykabutanError, allowing you to catch all library exceptions with a single handler.

docs.title: PykabutanError
docs.text:
class PykabutanError(Exception):
    """Base exception for all pykabutan errors."""

Base class for all pykabutan exceptions.

Example:

from pykabutan import PykabutanError

try:
    # Any pykabutan operation
    ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")
    profile = ticker.profile
except PykabutanError as e:
    print(f"pykabutan error: {e}")

docs.title: TickerNotFoundError
docs.text:
class TickerNotFoundError(PykabutanError):
    """Raised when a stock code doesn't exist on kabutan.jp."""

    code: str  # The invalid stock code

Raised when attempting to access a non-existent stock code.

Attributes:

Name Type Description code str The stock code that was not found

Example:

from pykabutan import TickerNotFoundError

try:
    ticker = pk.Ticker("9999999")
    profile = ticker.profile
except TickerNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Stock code not found: {e.code}")

docs.title: ScrapingError
docs.text:
class ScrapingError(PykabutanError):
    """Raised when scraping fails."""

    url: str  # The URL that failed

Raised when HTTP requests fail or HTML parsing encounters unexpected structure.

Attributes:

Name Type Description url str The URL that caused the error

Example:

from pykabutan import ScrapingError

try:
    results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")
except ScrapingError as e:
    print(f"Failed to scrape: {e.url}")

docs.title: ConfigurationError
docs.text:
class ConfigurationError(PykabutanError):
    """Raised when configuration is invalid."""

Raised when invalid configuration values are provided.

Example:

from pykabutan import ConfigurationError

try:
    pk.config.timeout = -1
except ValueError:
    # Note: Config validation raises ValueError, not ConfigurationError
    print("Invalid timeout value")

docs.title: Catching All Exceptions
docs.text:
import pykabutan as pk
from pykabutan import (
    PykabutanError,
    TickerNotFoundError,
    ScrapingError,
)

try:
    ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")
    profile = ticker.profile
except TickerNotFoundError as e:
    # Handle missing stock specifically
    print(f"Stock {e.code} not found")
except ScrapingError as e:
    # Handle scraping failures specifically
    print(f"Scraping failed for {e.url}")
except PykabutanError as e:
    # Catch any other pykabutan errors
    print(f"Unexpected error: {e}")

docs.title: Import
docs.text:
from pykabutan import (
    PykabutanError,
    TickerNotFoundError,
    ScrapingError,
    ConfigurationError,
)

Or import directly:

from pykabutan.exceptions import TickerNotFoundError

docs.title: Profile
docs.text: ::: pykabutan.profile.Profile

docs.title: Class Definition
docs.text:
@dataclass
class Profile:
    """Company profile data from kabutan.jp main page."""

docs.title: Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description code str Stock code name str \| None Company name (Japanese) market str \| None Market (e.g., "東証Ｐ") industry str \| None Industry classification description str \| None Company description themes list[str] \| None Related themes website str \| None Company website URL english_name str \| None English company name per float \| None Price-to-earnings ratio pbr float \| None Price-to-book ratio market_cap float \| None Market capitalization dividend_yield float \| None Dividend yield (%) margin_ratio float \| None Profit margin (%)

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Access profile through a Ticker:

import pykabutan as pk

ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")
profile = ticker.profile

print(profile.name)      # トヨタ自動車
print(profile.market)    # 東証Ｐ
print(profile.industry)  # 輸送用機器
print(profile.per)       # 10.5
print(profile.pbr)       # 1.2

docs.title: to_dict()
docs.text:
def to_dict(self) -> dict

Convert profile to dictionary.

Returns: dict with all profile fields

Example:

data = profile.to_dict()
print(data["name"])  # トヨタ自動車

docs.title: __iter__()
docs.text:
Profile supports iteration for dict conversion:

# Convert to dict using dict()
data = dict(profile)

# Iterate over fields
for name, value in profile:
    print(f"{name}: {value}")

docs.title: Missing Data
docs.text:
Fields that are not available on kabutan.jp return None:

# ETFs may have missing fields
ticker = pk.Ticker("1306")  # TOPIX ETF
profile = ticker.profile

print(profile.description)  # None (no description for ETF)
print(profile.per)          # None (N/A for ETF)

docs.title: search_by_industry
docs.text:
def search_by_industry(
    industry: str,
    market: str = "all"
) -> list[Ticker]

Search for stocks by industry classification.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description industry str required Industry name (Japanese) market str "all" Market filter

Returns: List of Ticker objects

Raises: ValueError if industry is not valid

Example:

import pykabutan as pk

# Search electronics industry
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

# Filter by market
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器", market="Prime")

# Access data
for t in results[:5]:
    print(t.code, t.profile.name)

docs.title: search_by_theme
docs.text:
def search_by_theme(
    theme: str,
    market: str = "all"
) -> list[Ticker]

Search for stocks by theme or keyword.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description theme str required Theme keyword (Japanese recommended) market str "all" Market filter

Returns: List of Ticker objects

Example:

import pykabutan as pk

# Search by theme (use Japanese for best results)
results = pk.search_by_theme("人工知能")
results = pk.search_by_theme("半導体")
results = pk.search_by_theme("EV")

# Access data
for t in results[:5]:
    print(t.code, t.profile.name)

docs.title: list_industries
docs.text:
def list_industries() -> list[str]

Get list of all available industry names.

Returns: List of 33 industry names (Japanese)

Example:

import pykabutan as pk

industries = pk.list_industries()
for industry in industries:
    print(industry)

docs.title: Working with Search Results
docs.text:
Search functions return Ticker objects:

results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

for t in results[:5]:
    print(t.code)           # Stock code (no HTTP request)
    print(t.profile.name)   # Company name (HTTP request)
    print(t.profile.market) # Market (cached after first access)
Access HTTP Request? Description t.code No Stock code available immediately t.profile.* Yes (first access) Profile data fetched on demand

docs.title: Market Values
docs.text:
Valid market filter values:

Value Aliases Description "all" "0" All markets "Prime" "東証Ｐ", "1" TSE Prime "Standard" "東証Ｓ", "2" TSE Standard "Growth" "東証Ｇ", "3" TSE Growth

docs.title: Industries
docs.text:
All 33 industry classifications:

Industry Industry 水産・農林業 鉱業 建設業 食料品 繊維製品 パルプ・紙 化学 医薬品 石油・石炭製品 ゴム製品 ガラス・土石製品 鉄鋼 非鉄金属 金属製品 機械 電気機器 輸送用機器 精密機器 その他製品 電気・ガス業 陸運業 海運業 空運業 倉庫・運輸関連業 情報・通信業 卸売業 小売業 銀行業 証券、商品先物取引業 保険業 その他金融業 不動産業 サービス業

docs.title: Ticker
docs.text: ::: pykabutan.Ticker

docs.title: Class Definition
docs.text:
class Ticker:
    """Represents a stock ticker on kabutan.jp."""

docs.title: Ticker(code)
docs.text:
Create a new Ticker instance.

Parameters:

Name Type Description code str or int Stock code (e.g., "7203" or 7203)

Example:

import pykabutan as pk

ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")
ticker = pk.Ticker(7203)  # Also accepts int

docs.title: code
docs.text:
@property
def code(self) -> str

The stock code as a string.

docs.title: profile
docs.text:
@property
def profile(self) -> Profile

Company profile information. See Profile for details.

Returns: Profile object

Raises: TickerNotFoundError if the stock code doesn't exist

docs.title: history()
docs.text:
def history(
    self,
    period: str = "30d",
    interval: str = "day",
    rows: int | None = None
) -> pd.DataFrame

Get historical price data.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description period str "30d" Time period: "7d", "30d", "90d", etc. interval str "day" Data interval: "day", "week", "month" rows int or None None Number of rows to return

Returns: pandas.DataFrame with columns:

  • date: Trading date
  • open: Opening price
  • high: High price
  • low: Low price
  • close: Closing price
  • volume: Trading volume
  • change: Price change

Example:

df = ticker.history(period="30d")
df = ticker.history(interval="week")
df = ticker.history(rows=100)

docs.title: news()
docs.text:
def news(self, news_type: str = "all") -> pd.DataFrame

Get company news.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description news_type str "all" Type: "all", "earnings", "general", "disclosure"

Returns: pandas.DataFrame with news articles

docs.title: financials()
docs.text:
def financials(self) -> pd.DataFrame

Get financial statements.

Returns: pandas.DataFrame with financial data

docs.title: holders()
docs.text:
def holders(self, holder_type: str = "major") -> pd.DataFrame

Get shareholder information.

Parameters:

Name Type Default Description holder_type str "major" Type: "major", "float", "treasury"

Returns: pandas.DataFrame with shareholder data

docs.title: similar_stocks()
docs.text:
def similar_stocks(self) -> list[SearchResult]

Get similar stocks.

Returns: List of SearchResult named tuples

docs.title: refresh()
docs.text:
def refresh(self) -> None

Clear cached data. Next property access will fetch fresh data.

Example:

ticker.refresh()
profile = ticker.profile  # Fresh data

docs.title: Configuration Guide
docs.text: pykabutan provides a global configuration object to customize request behavior.

docs.title: Accessing Config
docs.text:
import pykabutan as pk

# Access the global config
config = pk.config

docs.title: Timeout
docs.text:
Maximum time (in seconds) to wait for HTTP responses:

# Default: 30 seconds
print(pk.config.timeout)  # 30

# Set custom timeout
pk.config.timeout = 60

docs.title: Request Delay
docs.text:
Delay (in seconds) between consecutive HTTP requests:

# Default: 0.5 seconds
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 0.5

# Increase delay for more respectful scraping
pk.config.request_delay = 1.0

# Disable delay (not recommended)
pk.config.request_delay = 0

Rate Limiting

The request delay helps avoid overloading kabutan.jp servers. A value of 0.5-1.0 seconds is recommended.

docs.title: User Agent
docs.text:
HTTP User-Agent header sent with requests:

# Default: Chrome browser user agent
print(pk.config.user_agent)

# Set custom user agent
pk.config.user_agent = "MyApp/1.0"

docs.title: Reset to Defaults
docs.text:
Reset all configuration to default values:

pk.config.timeout = 120
pk.config.request_delay = 5.0

# Reset everything
pk.config.reset()

print(pk.config.timeout)        # 30
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 0.5

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
Configuration values are validated:

# Timeout must be positive
pk.config.timeout = 0    # Raises ValueError
pk.config.timeout = -1   # Raises ValueError

# Request delay must be non-negative
pk.config.request_delay = -1  # Raises ValueError

# User agent must be non-empty
pk.config.user_agent = ""    # Raises ValueError
pk.config.user_agent = None  # Raises ValueError

docs.title: Type Conversion
docs.text:
Values are automatically converted to the correct type:

# Float timeout is converted to int
pk.config.timeout = 30.5
print(pk.config.timeout)  # 30 (int)

# Int delay is converted to float
pk.config.request_delay = 2
print(pk.config.request_delay)  # 2.0 (float)

docs.title: Default Values
docs.text: Option Default Type Description timeout 30 int HTTP timeout in seconds request_delay 0.5 float Delay between requests user_agent Chrome UA str HTTP User-Agent header

docs.title: Example: Bulk Operations
docs.text:
When making many requests, increase the delay:

import pykabutan as pk

# Increase delay for bulk operations
pk.config.request_delay = 1.0

# Search multiple industries
industries = ["電気機器", "輸送用機器", "銀行業"]
all_results = []

for industry in industries:
    results = pk.search_by_industry(industry)
    all_results.extend(results)
    print(f"{industry}: {len(results)} stocks")

# Reset when done
pk.config.reset()

docs.title: Search Guide
docs.text: pykabutan provides functions to search for stocks by industry or theme.

docs.title: Search by Industry
docs.text:
Find stocks in a specific industry:

import pykabutan as pk

# Search electronics industry
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

print(f"Found {len(results)} stocks")
for t in results[:5]:
    print(f"{t.code}: {t.profile.name}")

docs.title: Available Industries
docs.text:
Japan has 33 standard industry classifications. List them with:

industries = pk.list_industries()
for industry in industries:
    print(industry)

Common industries include:

Japanese English 電気機器 Electronics 輸送用機器 Transportation Equipment 情報・通信業 Information & Communication 銀行業 Banking 医薬品 Pharmaceuticals 小売業 Retail サービス業 Services 化学 Chemicals 機械 Machinery 建設業 Construction

docs.title: Filter by Market
docs.text:
# Prime market only
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器", market="Prime")

# Standard market only
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器", market="Standard")

# Growth market only
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器", market="Growth")

Market options:

Value Description "Prime" or "東証Ｐ" Tokyo Stock Exchange Prime "Standard" or "東証Ｓ" Tokyo Stock Exchange Standard "Growth" or "東証Ｇ" Tokyo Stock Exchange Growth

docs.title: Search by Theme
docs.text:
Find stocks related to a specific theme or keyword:

# Search for AI-related stocks
results = pk.search_by_theme("人工知能")

# Search for EV-related stocks
results = pk.search_by_theme("EV")

# Search for semiconductor stocks
results = pk.search_by_theme("半導体")

Use Japanese Terms

Theme search works best with Japanese terms. While some English terms like "EV" work, Japanese terms like "人工知能" (AI) or "半導体" (semiconductor) yield better results.

docs.title: Filter by Market
docs.text:
# Prime market only
results = pk.search_by_theme("人工知能", market="Prime")

docs.title: Return Value
docs.text:
Both search functions return a list of Ticker objects:

results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

for t in results[:5]:
    print(t.code)          # Stock code (no HTTP request)
    print(t.profile.name)  # Company name (HTTP request)

docs.title: Accessing Profile Data
docs.text:
results = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")

# Each result is a Ticker object
ticker = results[0]

# Access full profile (triggers HTTP request)
print(ticker.profile.name)
print(ticker.profile.per)
print(ticker.profile.market)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
# Invalid industry raises ValueError
try:
    results = pk.search_by_industry("invalid_industry")
except ValueError as e:
    print(e)
    # "Unknown industry: invalid_industry. Available: ..."

docs.title: Rate Limiting
docs.text:
Search functions respect the configured rate limit:

# Increase delay between requests if needed
pk.config.request_delay = 1.0

# Then perform searches
results1 = pk.search_by_industry("電気機器")
results2 = pk.search_by_industry("銀行業")  # Waits 1 second before request

docs.title: Ticker Guide
docs.text: The Ticker class is the main interface for accessing stock data.

docs.title: Creating a Ticker
docs.text:
import pykabutan as pk

# Using string code
ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")

# Using integer code
ticker = pk.Ticker(7203)

Lazy Loading

Creating a Ticker does not make any HTTP requests. Data is fetched only when you access properties like profile or call methods like history().

docs.title: Profile
docs.text:
Access company profile information:

profile = ticker.profile

# Basic info
print(profile.code)     # 7203
print(profile.name)     # トヨタ自動車
print(profile.market)   # 東証Ｐ
print(profile.industry) # 輸送用機器

# Description and themes
print(profile.description)  # Company description
print(profile.themes)       # ['EV', '自動運転', ...]

# Financial metrics
print(profile.per)            # Price-to-earnings ratio
print(profile.pbr)            # Price-to-book ratio
print(profile.market_cap)     # Market capitalization
print(profile.dividend_yield) # Dividend yield
print(profile.margin_ratio)   # Profit margin

# Additional info
print(profile.website)      # Company website
print(profile.english_name) # English company name

docs.title: Convert to Dictionary
docs.text:
# Using dict()
data = dict(ticker.profile)

# Using to_dict()
data = ticker.profile.to_dict()

docs.title: Historical Prices
docs.text:
Get historical OHLCV data:

# Default: 30 days of daily data
df = ticker.history()

# Specify period
df = ticker.history(period="7d")   # Last 7 days
df = ticker.history(period="30d")  # Last 30 days
df = ticker.history(period="90d")  # Last 90 days

# Specify interval
df = ticker.history(interval="day")   # Daily (default)
df = ticker.history(interval="week")  # Weekly
df = ticker.history(interval="month") # Monthly

# Specify number of rows
df = ticker.history(rows=100)

# Combine options
df = ticker.history(period="90d", interval="week")

docs.title: DataFrame Columns
docs.text: Column Type Description date datetime64 Trading date open float Opening price high float High price low float Low price close float Closing price volume int Trading volume change float Price change

docs.title: News
docs.text:
Get company news:

# All news types
news = ticker.news()

# Filter by type
news = ticker.news(news_type="earnings")  # Earnings announcements
news = ticker.news(news_type="general")   # General news
news = ticker.news(news_type="disclosure") # Disclosures

docs.title: News DataFrame Columns
docs.text: Column Description date Publication date time Publication time title News headline url Link to full article

docs.title: Financials
docs.text:
Get financial statements:

df = ticker.financials()

Returns quarterly/annual financial data including revenue, profit, and other metrics.

docs.title: Shareholders
docs.text:
Get shareholder information:

# Major shareholders (default)
df = ticker.holders()

# Specify holder type
df = ticker.holders(holder_type="major")      # Major shareholders
df = ticker.holders(holder_type="float")      # Float shareholders
df = ticker.holders(holder_type="treasury")   # Treasury stock info

docs.title: Similar Stocks
docs.text:
Find stocks similar to this one:

similar = ticker.similar_stocks()

for t in similar:
    print(f"{t.code}: {t.profile.name}")

docs.title: Caching
docs.text:
Profile data is cached after the first access:

ticker = pk.Ticker("7203")

# First access: makes HTTP request
profile1 = ticker.profile

# Second access: uses cache (no HTTP request)
profile2 = ticker.profile

docs.title: Clearing Cache
docs.text:
# Clear cache and force refresh
ticker.refresh()

# Next access will make a new HTTP request
profile = ticker.profile

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
from pykabutan import TickerNotFoundError, ScrapingError

try:
    ticker = pk.Ticker("9999999")
    profile = ticker.profile
except TickerNotFoundError as e:
    print(f"Stock code not found: {e.code}")
except ScrapingError as e:
    print(f"Failed to scrape: {e.url}")
